Title: The trans-zeatin-type side-chain modification of cytokinins controls rice growth

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cytochrome P450 family CYP735A; P450 domain
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes trans-hydroxylation of the cytokinin side chain, converting iP-type cytokinins to tZ-type cytokinins
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: branch conversion step from iP-type to tZ-type cytokinins
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point for tZ-type cytokinin biosynthesis in rice
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes rice root and shoot growth, likely via meristem activity control
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes accumulation of tZ-type cytokinins and supports normal rice root and shoot growth
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Arabidopsis mutant complementation; CRISPR-Cas9 loss-of-function in rice; cytokinin metabolite profiling by UPLC-MS/MS; RT-qPCR; exogenous tZ rescue
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP735A3 complements the Arabidopsis cyp735a1 cyp735a2 mutant and restores tZ-type cytokinin accumulation. 2. Rice cyp735a3 cyp735a4 double mutants show a dramatic reduction of tZ-type cytokinins with reciprocal increase in iP-type cytokinins, demonstrating that CYP735A3 is required for tZ-type cytokinin biosynthesis. 3. CYP735A3 is expressed in both roots and shoots, with strong expression in roots and around the vegetative shoot apex. 4. Disruption together with CYP735A4 causes severe growth retardation in both roots and shoots, linking tZ-type cytokinin biosynthesis to growth promotion. 5. CYP735A3 expression is repressed by cytokinin, auxin, and ABA, but induced by ammonium/glutamine-related and nitrate-associated nitrogen signals.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Detected in roots and shoots of seedlings; more abundant in roots at seedling stage; highest around the vegetative shoot apex later; GUS in vascular parenchyma and pericycle-associated tissues
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in roots and shoots; nitrogen inducible in roots; downregulated by CK, IAA, and ABA
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S promoter (constitutive, in Arabidopsis complementation); native CYP735A3 promoter in GUS analysis
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: CK treatment (iP, tZ); auxin (IAA); ABA; ammonium; nitrate; glutamine; methionine sulfoximine; hydroponic nitrogen deprivation
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of CYP735A3 together with CYP735A4 causes reduced root and shoot growth, fewer tillers, delayed or failed heading, reduced panicle size and weight, and low seed set
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CYP735A3 responds to both glutamine-related and nitrate-specific nitrogen signals; residual tZ-type cytokinin in double mutants suggests minor alternative biosynthetic pathway(s), possibly cZ-to-tZ isomerization.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cytochrome P450 family CYP735A; P450 domain
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes trans-hydroxylation of the cytokinin side chain, converting iP-type cytokinins to tZ-type cytokinins
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: branch conversion step from iP-type to tZ-type cytokinins
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point for tZ-type cytokinin biosynthesis in rice
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes rice root and shoot growth, likely via meristem activity control
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes accumulation of tZ-type cytokinins and supports normal rice root and shoot growth
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Arabidopsis mutant complementation; CRISPR-Cas9 loss-of-function in rice; cytokinin metabolite profiling by UPLC-MS/MS; RT-qPCR; exogenous tZ rescue
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP735A4 complements the Arabidopsis cyp735a1 cyp735a2 mutant and restores tZ-type cytokinin accumulation. 2. Rice cyp735a4 single mutants show slight reduction in tZ-type cytokinins, whereas double mutants with cyp735a3 show dramatic tZ-type cytokinin deficiency, demonstrating shared essential function with CYP735A3. 3. CYP735A4 is expressed in roots and prominently in leaf blades at vegetative and early reproductive stages. 4. Combined loss with CYP735A3 causes strong growth retardation in roots and shoots. 5. CYP735A4 expression is repressed by cytokinin, auxin, and ABA, and is induced specifically by nitrate-related signaling.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Detected in roots and shoots; prominent in leaf blade during vegetative and early reproductive stages
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in roots and shoots; nitrate inducible in roots; downregulated by CK, IAA, and ABA
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S promoter (constitutive, in Arabidopsis complementation); native CYP735A4 upstream region tested for GUS but insufficient to drive detectable expression
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: CK treatment (iP, tZ); auxin (IAA); ABA; nitrate; methionine sulfoximine; hydroponic nitrogen deprivation
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Combined loss with CYP735A3 causes severe vegetative and reproductive growth defects
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CYP735A4 is nitrate-specific in its nitrogen response, unlike CYP735A3; 3.6 kb upstream sequence was insufficient for detectable GUS expression, implying additional cis-regulatory elements may be required.
